34bc3757057cbf679cdc861f48a1867e23140d9e.summary: iOS: Propagate VC UI preferences to SwiftUI hosting controller
34bc3757057cbf679cdc861f48a1867e23140d9e.body:
The SwiftUI lifecycle wraps GDTViewController in a hosting controller
that becomes the window's root VC. iOS queries the root VC for
preferredScreenEdgesDeferringSystemGestures, prefersHomeIndicatorAutoHidden,
and prefersStatusBarHidden, but the hosting controller doesn't delegate
these to child VCs. This propagates the preference implementations onto
the root VC's class at runtime using class_addMethod.

(cherry picked from commit 0c8c425f9be73b3ff43b96e9998edb3608b743a0)

0c8c425f9be73b3ff43b96e9998edb3608b743a0.summary: iOS: Propagate VC UI preferences to SwiftUI hosting controller
0c8c425f9be73b3ff43b96e9998edb3608b743a0.body:
The SwiftUI lifecycle wraps GDTViewController in a hosting controller
that becomes the window's root VC. iOS queries the root VC for
preferredScreenEdgesDeferringSystemGestures, prefersHomeIndicatorAutoHidden,
and prefersStatusBarHidden, but the hosting controller doesn't delegate
these to child VCs. This propagates the preference implementations onto
the root VC's class at runtime using class_addMethod.

7a18370d44ceb32b0db2515498c6bd4a974d7760.summary: [Apple Embedded] Fix static .a/.xcframework library loading in open_dynamic_library
7a18370d44ceb32b0db2515498c6bd4a974d7760.body:
When a .a or .xcframework path is requested but not found on disk (because
it is already statically linked into the binary), the previous code set
`path = String()` and fell through to `dlopen("")`, which is undefined
behavior and bypasses the RTLD_SELF symbol lookup table used by
`get_dynamic_library_symbol_handle`. This caused GDExtension loading to
fail silently or with ERR_CANT_OPEN when building with vulkan=no on iOS.

Fix by returning RTLD_SELF early, consistent with the existing empty-path
case at the top of the function.

(cherry picked from commit 44541235bac60be76a2801eea9e411a2b116c33b)

44541235bac60be76a2801eea9e411a2b116c33b.summary: [Apple Embedded] Fix static .a/.xcframework library loading in open_dynamic_library
44541235bac60be76a2801eea9e411a2b116c33b.body:
When a .a or .xcframework path is requested but not found on disk (because
it is already statically linked into the binary), the previous code set
`path = String()` and fell through to `dlopen("")`, which is undefined
behavior and bypasses the RTLD_SELF symbol lookup table used by
`get_dynamic_library_symbol_handle`. This caused GDExtension loading to
fail silently or with ERR_CANT_OPEN when building with vulkan=no on iOS.

Fix by returning RTLD_SELF early, consistent with the existing empty-path
case at the top of the function.

cd9d78acfce5597b0ce4453cc0a79b382ff7e1d0.summary: Fix UPNP code sample using `OK` constant incorrectly
cd9d78acfce5597b0ce4453cc0a79b382ff7e1d0.body:
The returned value is an UPNPResult, not an Error. Both
`UPNP.UPNP_RESULT_SUCCESS` and `OK` are equal to 0, so it will
behave the same, but it's not identical from a static typing perspective.

(cherry picked from commit cfa8d762ef8c513faa73b7f08074858475127921)

The returned value is an UPNPResult, not an Error. Both
`UPNP.UPNP_RESULT_SUCCESS` and `OK` are equal to 0, so it will
behave the same, but it's not identical from a static typing perspective.

d8d4e7cb7c88425a2885e7ee2a158e48509ea5f0.summary: Fix detection of some features in Engine Compilation Configuration Editor
d8d4e7cb7c88425a2885e7ee2a158e48509ea5f0.body:
A copy-paste error led to some incorrect feature detections,
specifically for X11, Wayland and Direct3D 12.

This adds blank lines between options to reduce the risk of future typos.

795a61dbb3a4983d48b0bd64bc4d63fbcd49d6dd.summary: Fixing shader compilation error when writing to FOG when render_mode fog_disabled.
795a61dbb3a4983d48b0bd64bc4d63fbcd49d6dd.body:
Always declares FOG variable regardless of fog state. Previously, only declared
fog if FOG_DISABLED was undefined. This caused errors if the code tried to
write to fog later. Exists as a dummy variable when unused.

Signed-off-by: Joshua Rothe <joshrothe@gmail.com>
(cherry picked from commit 7a2f188382ecbe7f81a4378e9786e0da0b575f9f)

7a2f188382ecbe7f81a4378e9786e0da0b575f9f.summary: Fixing shader compilation error when writing to FOG when render_mode fog_disabled.
7a2f188382ecbe7f81a4378e9786e0da0b575f9f.body:
Always declares FOG variable regardless of fog state. Previously, only declared
fog if FOG_DISABLED was undefined. This caused errors if the code tried to
write to fog later. Exists as a dummy variable when unused.

Signed-off-by: Joshua Rothe <joshrothe@gmail.com>

997cdc1e3fa5a47f30c62c32aa954812a8b3dfb4.summary: Jolt Physics: Swapping vertices of triangle if it is scaled inside out
997cdc1e3fa5a47f30c62c32aa954812a8b3dfb4.body:
Fixed an issue where collide/cast shape against a triangle would return a hit result with mShape2Face in incorrect winding order. This caused an incorrect normal in the enhanced internal edge removal algorithm. This in turn resulted in objects not settling properly on dense triangle grids.

(cherry picked from commit d0025bd4a3bba845f0d4c04869447bcb17fa6a02)

d0025bd4a3bba845f0d4c04869447bcb17fa6a02.summary: Jolt Physics: Swapping vertices of triangle if it is scaled inside out
d0025bd4a3bba845f0d4c04869447bcb17fa6a02.body: Fixed an issue where collide/cast shape against a triangle would return a hit result with mShape2Face in incorrect winding order. This caused an incorrect normal in the enhanced internal edge removal algorithm. This in turn resulted in objects not settling properly on dense triangle grids.

8054ff5d03bb9775dfee16d598e8db8a627abf5c.summary: Wayland: Skip resize request when the size is the same
8054ff5d03bb9775dfee16d598e8db8a627abf5c.body:
`DisplayServer::window_set_size` is called lots of times in the code,
with the assumption (I suppose) that it's going to be idempotent.

We had checks in _update_window_rect but we still called
`WaylandThread::window_set_size`, which did a lot of stuff. In
particular, this caused issues with HiDPI as it "overrode" the window
size before it had a time to figure out its scale.

(cherry picked from commit 870631211f507b302b3fbc289a62891115ad7a29)

870631211f507b302b3fbc289a62891115ad7a29.summary: Wayland: Skip resize request when the size is the same
870631211f507b302b3fbc289a62891115ad7a29.body:
`DisplayServer::window_set_size` is called lots of times in the code,
with the assumption (I suppose) that it's going to be idempotent.

We had checks in _update_window_rect but we still called
`WaylandThread::window_set_size`, which did a lot of stuff. In
particular, this caused issues with HiDPI as it "overrode" the window
size before it had a time to figure out its scale.

e07be1c9bda2679194af4156fbac95c8f7aab789.summary: Fix EditorSettings error due to `android_sdk_path` when exporting a project
e07be1c9bda2679194af4156fbac95c8f7aab789.body:
This editor setting is queried once every 3 seconds, as it is used
to check whether devices have been (dis)connected for one-click deploy.

This method may be called early on depending on initialization order,
which has led to occasional error messages when exporting a project.
The method now returns early if EditorSettings isn't ready yet
(it will be called again soon after anyway).

(cherry picked from commit 7d6d11f8d8a6c39d8077fe80937e5c000d1f832c)

7d6d11f8d8a6c39d8077fe80937e5c000d1f832c.summary: Fix EditorSettings error due to `android_sdk_path` when exporting a project
7d6d11f8d8a6c39d8077fe80937e5c000d1f832c.body:
This editor setting is queried once every 3 seconds, as it is used
to check whether devices have been (dis)connected for one-click deploy.

This method may be called early on depending on initialization order,
which has led to occasional error messages when exporting a project.
The method now returns early if EditorSettings isn't ready yet
(it will be called again soon after anyway).

539c06f94d935cd5caf521a3cb8fb68d45c00eed.summary: Wayland: Only handle the current output mode
539c06f94d935cd5caf521a3cb8fb68d45c00eed.body:
Some compositors (e.g. COSMIC) can report *all* supported output modes,
not just the current mode. This is valid, albeit deprecated, so let's
add a check and ignore any non-current output events.

(cherry picked from commit dc8e7ef2ce5deb8353a688951e9686f4510629a6)

add a check and ignore any non-current output events.
